_meta.coverage: Clauses 6.1 to 7.2.2 and Tables 1-6 (from supplied screenshots, pp. 6-20)
_meta.encoding_note: Numeric values, formulas and tables are verbatim/exact (factual, checker-readable). Prose rules are stated concisely, not reproduced verbatim from the standard.
_meta.schema_note: Field names should be aligned to file_for_DBR.json if that corpus uses different keys (e.g. 'text' vs 'statement'). Object shape kept flat for easy merge.

clauses.title: Soil-Structure Interaction
clauses.statement: SSI accounts for flexibility of the supporting soil-foundation system on structural response. It may be neglected for structures founded on rock or rock-like material at shallow depth.

clauses.title: Equipment and Supported Systems
clauses.statement: Equipment/systems supported at various floor levels experience different motions at their support points; floor response spectra may be needed for their design. Reference IS 1893 (Part 4).

clauses.title: Additions to Existing Structures
clauses.statement: A structurally independent addition is designed as a new structure. A structurally connected addition must make the whole conform to new-structure seismic requirements, unless: (1) the addition complies with new-structure requirements; (2) it does not increase seismic forces in any existing element by more than 5 percent (unless that element still complies); and (3) it does not decrease the seismic resistance of any existing element (unless reduced resistance still meets new-structure requirement).

clauses.title: Change in Occupancy
clauses.statement: If a change of occupancy re-classifies a structure to a higher importance factor I, the structure shall conform to new-structure seismic requirements for the higher I.

clauses.title: Assumptions
clauses.statement: Earthquake-resistant design assumes: (a) ground motions are complex, irregular and short-duration so steady-state resonance is not normally expected (exceptions exist for long-distance waves on tall structures on deep soft soils); (b) earthquake is not concurrent with high wind, max flood or max sea waves; (c) elastic modulus of materials taken as for static analysis unless more definite dynamic values are available (see IS 456, IS 800, IS 1343, IS 1905, IS 2974).

clauses.title: Load Combinations
clauses.statement: Load combinations from respective standards apply; in addition the earthquake combinations of this standard apply. Per 6.3.1.1, where non-earthquake combinations give larger demands, the design/ductile-detailing provisions of this standard, IS 13920 and IS 800 still apply.

clauses.title: Design Horizontal Earthquake Load - Orthogonal Systems
clauses.statement: When lateral-load-resisting elements are oriented along two mutually orthogonal directions, the structure is designed for full design earthquake load in one horizontal direction at a time (not both simultaneously).

clauses.title: Design Horizontal Earthquake Load - Non-Orthogonal Systems
clauses.statement: When lateral elements are NOT orthogonal (per 7.1 and Table 5(e)), design for full EQ load in one direction plus 30 percent in the other: (a) +-EL_X +- 0.3 EL_Y, and (b) +-0.3 EL_X +- EL_Y.

clauses.title: Design Vertical Earthquake Effects - When Required
clauses.statement: Vertical earthquake shaking effects shall be considered when any apply: (a) Seismic Zone IV or V; (b) vertical or plan irregularities; (c) rested on soft soil; (d) bridges; (e) long spans; (f) large horizontal overhangs of members/sub-systems.

clauses.title: Design Vertical Force
clauses.statement: When vertical shaking is considered, the design vertical force is computed for vertical ground motion as per 6.4.6.

clauses.title: Three-Directional Combination - 30 Percent Rule
clauses.statement: When responses from three components are considered, when max response from one component occurs the other two are 30 percent each of their maximum. All sign combinations of (EL_X, EL_Y, EL_Z) considered: (a) +-EL_X +-0.3 EL_Y +-0.3 EL_Z; (b) +-EL_Y +-0.3 EL_Z +-0.3 EL_X; (c) +-EL_Z +-0.3 EL_X +-0.3 EL_Y. X,Y orthogonal plan; Z vertical.

clauses.title: Three-Directional Combination - SRSS Alternative
clauses.statement: Alternative to 6.3.4.1: net response EL = sqrt(EL_X^2 + EL_Y^2 + EL_Z^2). Caution: loses sign of axial/shear/moment; can be grossly uneconomical.

clauses.title: Increase in Net Pressure on Soils - Unfactored Loads
clauses.statement: In foundation design, unfactored loads shall be combined in line with IS 2974 while assessing bearing pressure in soils.

clauses.title: Permissible Increase in Net Bearing Pressure
clauses.statement: When earthquake forces are included, net bearing pressure may be increased per Table 1 by foundation type and soil type (Table 2). In soft soils no increase is permitted because settlements cannot be restricted by raising bearing pressure.

clauses.title: Liquefaction Screening
clauses.statement: For submerged loose sands and SP soils with corrected SPT N less than 15 in Zones III/IV/V (and less than 10 in Zone II), earthquake shaking may cause liquefaction or excessive total/differential settlement. Such sites should be avoided for important projects; otherwise investigate settlements and adopt compaction/stabilization to reach Note-4 N values, or use deep piles anchored below liquefiable layers. Marine/sensitive clays also need special treatment. Annex F gives a simplified liquefaction-potential method.

clauses.title: Percentage Increase in Net Bearing Pressure and Skin Friction of Soils

notes.1: Net bearing pressure determined per IS 6403 or IS 1888.
notes.2: Only corrected values of N shall be used.
notes.3: Total increase (seismic + any prior non-seismic increase) shall not exceed limits above.

clauses.title: Classification of Soils for Net Bearing Pressure / Skin Friction Increase

data.Type A (Rock/hard): GW/SW with <5% fines; GW-SW mixtures; SP or SC with N>30; stiff-to-hard clays N>30 (corrected SPT).
data.Type B (Medium/stiff): SP poorly graded sands/with gravel, little/no fines, N 10-30; ML or low-compressibility CL clays, N 10-30.
data.Type C (Soft): All soft soils other than SP with N<10 (MI, MH, CI, CH, MI-MH/CI-CH, MI-CI, MH-CH).
data.Type D (Unstable): Unstable, collapsible, liquefiable soils - require site-specific study and special treatment (see 6.3.5.3).

clauses.title: Seismic Zones
clauses.statement: For determining design seismic force the country is classified into four seismic zones (II, III, IV, V) as shown in Fig. 1.

variables.I: Importance factor (IS 1893 Parts 1-5); minimum if unspecified: 1.5 critical/lifeline, 1.2 business-continuity, 1.0 rest
variables.Sa/g: Design acceleration coefficient by soil type vs natural period T (Fig. 2 / branches below); default 5 percent damping

clauses.title: Soil Type for Selecting Sa/g Spectrum
clauses.statement: Soil type for (Sa/g) per Table 4: I Rock/hard, II Medium/stiff, III Soft. N taken as weighted average of corrected N over soil layers from existing ground level to 30 m below.

clauses.title: Classification of Soils for the Design Earthquake Spectrum

data.I - Rock or Hard: GW or SW with <5% fines; GW-SW with/without fines; SP or SC all with N>30; stiff-to-hard clays N>30.
data.II - Medium or Stiff: SP poorly graded sands / with gravel, little/no fines, N 10-30; ML stiff-to-medium-stiff fine soils or low-compressibility CL clays N 10-30.
data.III - Soft: All soft soils other than SP with N<10 (MI, MH, CI, CH, MI-MH/CI-CH, MI-CI, MH-CH).

clauses.title: Analysis Methods
clauses.statement: Design earthquake load effects considered via (a) Equivalent Static Method, or (b) Dynamic Analysis Method (Response Spectrum / Modal Time History / Time History). Equivalent static may be used for regular structures with approximate natural period Ta less than 0.4 s.

clauses.title: Cracked Section / Moment of Inertia for Analysis
clauses.statement: For structural analysis the moment of inertia shall be taken as: (a) RC and masonry structures - 70 percent of Igross for columns and 35 percent of Igross for beams; (b) steel structures - Igross of both beams and columns.

clauses.title: Ah per Mode (Response Spectrum)
clauses.statement: Where multiple modes are considered, Ah per 6.4.2 for each mode k is determined using that mode's natural period Tk.

clauses.title: Underground / Deep Structures
clauses.statement: For underground structures and buildings whose base is at depth 30 m or more, Ah at base = half the 6.4.2 value (for inertia of masses at corresponding below-ground levels); above-ground portion uses unreduced Ah. Between ground level and 30 m depth, interpolate linearly between Ah and 0.5 Ah.

clauses.title: Design Vertical Seismic Acceleration (Av)
clauses.statement: Design vertical acceleration spectral value Av. Sa/g based on natural period T of the first vertical mode, using 6.4.2.

clauses.title: Buildings - Desirable Attributes
clauses.statement: Four desirable attributes of an earthquake-resistant building: (a) robust structural configuration; (b) at least a minimum elastic lateral stiffness; (c) at least a minimum lateral strength; (d) adequate ductility.

clauses.title: Regular and Irregular Configurations
clauses.statement: Buildings with simple regular geometry and uniformly distributed mass/stiffness suffer less damage. A building is irregular if any condition in Tables 5 or 6 applies. Limits on irregularities for Zones III/IV/V are in Tables 5 and 6.

clauses.title: Definitions of Irregular Buildings - Plan Irregularities

i_torsional.definition: Torsionally irregular when max horizontal displacement at one end of a floor > 1.5 x min horizontal displacement at the far end (same floor, same direction); OR fundamental torsional mode period exceeds those of the first two translational modes along each principal direction.
i_torsional.limits: Ratio 1.5-2.0: (a) revise config so torsional mode period < first two translational mode periods, then (b) 3D dynamic analysis. Ratio > 2.0: configuration shall be revised.

ii_reentrant_corners.definition: Re-entrant corner when a plan projection > 15 percent of overall plan dimension in that direction.

iii_floor_slab_cutouts.definition: Discontinuity in in-plane stiffness when floor-slab cut-outs/openings > 50 percent of full floor-slab area.
iii_floor_slab_cutouts.action: Cut-out <=50%: slab rigid or flexible per location/size of openings. Cut-out >50%: slab taken as flexible.

iv_out_of_plane_offsets.definition: Out-of-plane offset when structural walls/frames are moved out of plane in any storey along the height.
iv_out_of_plane_offsets.action: Zone II: refer specialist literature. Zones III/IV/V: (1) lateral drift < 0.2 percent in storey with offset and storeys below; and (2) refer specialist literature to remove the irregularity.

v_non_parallel_system.definition: Non-parallel system when vertical lateral-force-resisting systems are not oriented along the two principal orthogonal plan axes.
v_non_parallel_system.action: Analyze for load combinations of 6.3.2.2 or 6.3.4.1.

clauses.title: Definitions of Irregular Buildings - Vertical Irregularities

i_stiffness_soft_storey.definition: Soft storey: a storey whose lateral stiffness is less than that of the storey above.
i_stiffness_soft_storey.action: Estimate structural plan density (SPD) when URM infills used. If SPD of masonry infills > 20 percent, model infills as equivalent diagonal struts (per 7.9); design RC members for the larger of bare-frame and URM-infill (3D) analysis. If designed considering URM infills, inter-storey drift limited to 0.2 percent in the stiffening storey and all storeys below.

ii_mass_irregularity.definition: Mass irregularity when seismic weight (per 7.7) of any floor > 150 percent of that of floors below.
ii_mass_irregularity.action: Zones III/IV/V: estimate earthquake effects by Dynamic Analysis Method (per 7.7).

iii_vertical_geometric.definition: Vertical geometric irregularity when horizontal dimension of the lateral-force-resisting system in any storey > 125 percent of the storey below.

iv_in_plane_discontinuity.definition: In-plane discontinuity when in-plane offset of lateral elements > 20 percent of the plan length of those elements.
iv_in_plane_discontinuity.action: Zone II: lateral drift limited to 0.2 percent of building height. Zones III/IV/V: buildings with in-plane discontinuity NOT permitted.

v_strength_weak_storey.definition: Weak storey: a storey whose lateral strength is less than that of the storey above.
v_strength_weak_storey.action: Zones III/IV/V: design so building safety is not jeopardized; provisions of 7.10 shall be followed.

vi_floating_stub_columns.definition: Floating/stub columns likely cause concentrated damage.
vi_floating_stub_columns.action: Undesirable; prohibited if part of or supporting the primary lateral load resisting system.

vii_irregular_modes.definition: Irregular modes of oscillation in a principal plan direction when: (a) first three modes contribute < 65 percent mass participation in each principal direction, AND (b) fundamental lateral periods in the two principal directions are within 10 percent of the larger.
vii_irregular_modes.action: Zones II/III: ensure first three modes together contribute >= 65 percent mass in each direction. Zones IV/V: ensure (1) first three modes >= 65 percent mass each direction, and (2) the two fundamental periods differ by at least 10 percent of the larger value.

clauses.title: Design Lateral Force (Base Shear)
clauses.statement: Buildings designed for design lateral force VB = Ah W, with Ah per 6.4.2 and W (seismic weight) per 7.4.

clauses.title: Minimum Design Lateral Force
clauses.statement: Regardless of design earthquake force per 7.3.1, buildings shall have lateral-load-resisting systems capable of resisting a horizontal force not less than (VB)min given in Table 7.
